section.heading: Medical standards
section.chapeau: The owner of a vessel to which section 3507 applies shall ensure that—

paragraph.content: a physician is always present and available to treat any passengers who may be on board the vessel in the event of an emergency situation;
the vessel is in compliance with the Health Care Guidelines for Cruise Ship Medical Facilities established by the American College of Emergency Physicians; and

paragraph.chapeau: the initial safety briefing given to the passengers on board the vessel includes—

subparagraph.content: the location of the vessel’s medical facilities; and
the appropriate steps passengers should follow during a medical emergency.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 116–283, div. G, title LVXXXIII [LXXXIII], § 8322(a), Jan. 1, 2021, 134 Stat. 4702.)

note.heading: Exemptions for Certain Passenger Vessels
note.p: For provisions exempting certain passenger vessels from the requirements authorized under this section, see section 11510 of Pub. L. 117–263, set out as a note under section 3508 of this title.
